micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Distinction: Only Light (Rabbinically Impure) Fluids Permit Waiting for Emergence

macro_segments.title: Rashi's Dual Argument: Proving Non-Blood and Rendering Stains Non-Present

micro_segments.title: Rashi's First Argument: Cleansers Failing to Remove a Stain Indicates It Is Not Blood
Rashi's Second Argument: Cleansers Failing to Remove Shows the Stain Is Not Considered Present

Resolution via Temporal Distinction: Presence Only When Blood Is Not Yet Absorbed

micro_segments.title: Cannot Emerge and Not Concerned: Universally Permitted (Tahur) Regardless of Status
Can Emerge and Concerned: Universally Prohibited (Tamei) Regardless of Status
Can Emerge but Not Concerned: The Debate Between Reish Lakish (Always Tamei) and Rabbi Yochanan (Permitted if Non-Biblical or Paled)
